reason: INHERITED UNPROVENANCED COMPLETENESS (round-1 ch-66480791e54975d9 only partially resolved). Node 1.3's operations -- 'R=f(S^2) is well-defined', 'R is a norm-limit of polynomials in S^2', and taking the limit through multiplication -- all require that End(B(H)_sa) be a COMPLETE Banach algebra, which 1.3 imports via its declared dependency on node 1.1.2. But node 1.1.2's completeness justification 'It is moreover a Banach algebra (B(H)_sa is a complete order-unit space, GT-order-unit-norm)' is NOT supported by the cited locus. Byte-match of GT-order-unit-norm = refs/hos/joa-m.md:368-372: line 372 only DEFINES the term ('If A is furthermore a Banach space with respect to the order norm then A is called a complete order unit space') -- it nowhere asserts that B(H)_sa IS complete in the order norm, and HOS 1.2.1 is a general definition not a statement about B(H)_sa. (For finite-dim H this is true but still needs a refs/ source under the no-standard-facts rule; for general H the order/operator norm completeness of B(H)_sa likewise needs provenance.) Consequently every existence/limit operation in 1.3 rests on an ungrounded completeness premise. Either node 1.1.2 must provenance completeness of B(H)_sa (and hence of End(B(H)_sa) as a Banach algebra) against a valid refs/ source, or 1.3 must not rely on it. Note: the joint-continuity sub-claim itself (RS=lim p_N(S^2)S=lim S p_N(S^2)=SR) is now adequately cited to Kitaev:642; the remaining defect is completeness, not submultiplicativity.
